_description: Cataract surgery in 72-year-old with dual wallet (family + Vay Vandana). Tests VAY_VANDANA_DEBIT_ORDER_AMBIGUOUS flag in Phase 5 and PAEDIATRIC_DEVICE flag should NOT fire (age 72, not paediatric). Day-care package.

clinical.chief_complaints: Gradual painless diminution of vision right eye over 2 years, difficulty reading and recognising faces, glare in bright light
clinical.history_of_present_illness: Patient noticed progressive painless decrease in vision in the right eye over the past 2 years. Difficulty in near vision and recognising faces. Glare while driving at night. No redness, no pain, no discharge. Left eye vision relatively better. Patient is a known hypertensive and diabetic.
clinical.provisional_diagnosis: Senile cataract right eye — mature nuclear sclerosis
clinical.planned_procedure: Phacoemulsification with foldable IOL implantation right eye

examination_findings.general: Elderly female, conscious, oriented, mild pallor, no icterus
examination_findings.local: Right eye: BCVA 6/60 not improving with pinhole. Dense nuclear cataract on slit lamp. Fundus view hazy. Left eye: BCVA 6/18 with glasses. Mild cortical cataract.

investigations.result_summary: Ocular biometry (A-scan): Axial length RE 23.4mm, IOL power calculated +20.5D. Keratometry: 43.5/44.0 D. BCVA right eye: 6/60, does not improve with pinhole or refraction. Slit lamp: Dense nuclear sclerosis grade 3+. IOP: 14 mmHg bilaterally.
Pre-op workup: Hb 11.2 g/dL, blood glucose fasting 142 mg/dL, blood glucose PP 198 mg/dL, HbA1c 7.8%

clinical.past_medical_history: Hypertension 15 years, Type 2 DM 12 years, both on medication. Prior cataract surgery left eye 5 years ago at another hospital (not under PM-JAY).
clinical.past_surgical_history: Cataract surgery left eye 5 years ago
clinical.family_history: No significant family history of glaucoma or retinal disease
clinical.notes: Patient pre-op clearance done. Blood pressure controlled on medication. Blood sugar acceptable for surgery. Informed consent obtained. Planned for phacoemulsification under local anaesthesia.
